channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: One entry amended on UN ISIL sanctions list
item.description: Hamida Nabaggala’s entry on the UN ISIL and Al-Qaeda sanctions list has been amended. Ms Nabaggala was listed by the UN in March 2026 for operating as a financier for ISIL in Central Africa. The amend

item.title: Thailand brings dual-use goods under export licensing control
item.description: Thailand's Ministry of Commerce has issued two announcements that place dual-use items under export control. The first announcement designates the dual-use items that need a licence to export or re

item.title: US begins process of rescinding Syria’s ‘state sponsor of terrorism’ designation
item.description: The US has begun the process of rescinding Syria’s designation as a state sponsor of terrorism. SST designation is made under 3 US laws: section 1754(c) of the National Defense Authorization Act fo

item.title: UK HMRC issues sanctions technical note
item.description: HMRC, the UK’s tax authority, has published technical notes on its 2025/26 sanctions enforcement activity. HMRC is responsible for enforcing trade sanctions on goods moving to or from the UK, the t

item.title: EU Commission sends reasoned opinion to Poland over failure to transpose sanctions criminalisation directive
item.description: The European Commission has sent a reasoned opinion to Poland (INFR(2025)0239) for failing to fully transpose the Directive on the criminalisation of the violation of EU sanctions (Directive (EU) 2024

item.title: EU General Court rejects Deripaska’s delisting application
item.description: The EU General Court has rejected Oleg Deripaska’s application to annul his September 2024, March 2025 and September 2025 relistings under Regulation (EU) 269/2014 – Case T-606/24. Mr Deripaska was

item.title: UK enacts National Security (State Threats) Act
item.description: The UK has enacted the National Security (State Threats) Act 2026. The Act allows the UK Home Secretary to designate bodies engaged in state threat activity, equivalent to proscription under the Terro

item.title: US renews Russia GL authorising some transactions with the Russian Central Bank, National Wealth Funds & Finance Ministry until 9 October 2026
item.description: The US has issued General License 13R to continue to authorise transactions involving the Russian Central Bank, National Wealth Fund and Ministry of Finance which would otherwise be prohibited by Dir

item.title: Latvian Financial Intelligence Unit publishes guidance on assessing ‘control’ exercised by designated public officials
item.description: The Latvian Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) has published updated guidance on the meaning and application of the ‘control test’ under EU targeted financial sanctions, where control might be exercise

item.title: CJEU rejects Nathalie Yamb’s appeal against refusal of interim measures in Russia hybrid threats listing challenge
item.description: The European Court of Justice has dismissed an appeal by Nathalie Yamb against a General Court order refusing to suspend her EU sanctions listing pending final hearing – Yamb v Council, C-29/26 P(R).
